replies.msg:
Dim Sum One, one of the most sought-after Dim Sum restaurants in town, is praised for its contribution to the Hong Kong protests. The “One” in “Dim Sum One” stands for “Not One Less” from the chant “Five Demands Not One Less”. The eatery hires protestors and donates part of its proceeds to online news distributor Stand News and 612 Humanitarian Relief Fund on a monthly basis.

My favourite dish is this scrumptious “Five Vegetable Ball” – or “Five Demands”. Deep-fried in Japanese sesame oil and filled generously with five different kinds of locally grown veggies, the golden yellow balls are crispy and fragrant. Its melt-in-your-mouth crust is lip-smacking and satiates cravings for junk food.

The service here is top-notch, too. Waiters are patient and hospitable, going to great lengths to provide customers with the best experience they can ever have within and beyond the yellow economic circle. Do remember to shout “Liberate Hong Kong” if you are ready to order, as the waiters will shout “The Revolution of Our Times” back and you get an instant 10% discount.

Dim Sum One is best for any occasions – the restaurant is also pet-friendly, although they make it clear that no cops and Mainland Chinese are allowed.

With no doubt, Dim Sum One deserves a solid 10/10. Do give it a try over the upcoming Golden Week in May!

Dim Sum One, renouned for its unweavering support for the Hong Kong protests, is one of the most sought-after Dim Sum restaurants in town. The “One” in “Dim Sum One” represents “Not One Less” from protestor's slogan- “Five Demands Not One Less”. Notably, the eatery adopts a policy of hiring protestors and donates part of its proceeds to online news publisher Stand News and 612 Humanitarian Relief Fund on a monthly basis.

The most popular dish is the scrumptious “Five Vegetable Balls”, also known as “Five Demands”. Deep-fried in Japanese sesame oil and filled generously with five different kinds of locally grown veggies, the golden balls are crispy and fragrant. Its melt-in-your-mouth crust is lip-smacking and satiates cravings for junk food.

Service here is top-notch, creme of the creme. Waiters are well-informed, patient and hospitable. The eatery went the extra-mile to provide customers with sublime experiences that they can ever desire within and beyond the yellow economic circle. Do remember to shout “Liberate Hong Kong” when ready to order, the waiters will reply with “The Revolution of Our Times”; doing so will grant you a 10% discount.

Dim Sum One is best for any occasions – the restaurant is pet-friendly; although they make it clear that the police and Mainland Chinese are barred from entry.

With no doubt, Dim Sum One is exceptional and highly recommended. Do give it a try over the upcoming Golden Week in May!

Dim Sum One is best for any occasions – the restaurant is also pet-friendly, although they make it clear that no cops and Mainland Chinese are allowed.

replies.msg: I take that you must have misread my comment. 'Vegeterian' in its literal and formal adoption entails a meaning of dishes made exclusively (100%) in vegetable, served for vegeterians. It is not only a mere description but also a statement of trust and quality. Thus to qualify as vegeterian, the dish must be made in the aforementioned way- fit for purpose, otherwise it would be a misrepresentation. On the contrary, 'vegetable' carriers a simple description of the ingredients and as such is not subjected to the rigourous standards. The adoption of the latter would encompass the former but the adoption of the former caqnnot be said the same. The trival differences might be insignificant to some, but the devil is in the details.
